title: caltha palustris subsp. sibirica
intro: Caltha palustris subsp. sibirica, also known as Sibirian marsh-marigold, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the buttercup family, Ranunculaceae. It is a perennial herb with yellow flowers that are used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions. The medicinal properties of Caltha palustris subsp. sibirica are attributed to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds, which have been shown to have health benefits such as reducing fever and relieving digestive issues. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat colds, flu, and other infections.
